# Component Partition — Substrate / Thing / Doc / View

## The mental model

JOS has four kinds of components. Every component lives in exactly
one kind. The four are not stacked — they are partitioned.

| Bucket | Cardinality | Examples |
|---|---|---|
| **Substrate** | ~10 kinds | Rule, Principle, Standard, Method, Spec, Concept, Contract, Decision, Gate, Monitor (+ Diagnostic, Playbook, Guide, Kit, etc.) |
| **Thing** | 14 kinds | Venture, System, Subsystem, Product, Client-Service, Internal-Tooling, Kit, Library-Bundle, Library-Atom, Client-Engagement, Client-Deliverable, Single-Purpose-Script, Incubator, Hosted-Venture |
| **Doc** | 11 kinds | OVERVIEW, README, PURPOSE, VISION, ROADMAP, DECISION-LOG, CHANGELOG, ARCHITECTURE, GUARANTEES, HEALTH-STANDARD, WDGLL |
| **View** | 4 kinds | Registry, Index, Manifest, Directory |

The four buckets are **mutually exclusive** (a Method is a Substrate
component; it is not also a Thing or a Doc or a View) and
**collectively exhaustive** (every component JOS knows about lands in
one of them). MECE.

## What each bucket IS

### Substrate (the structural primitives)

Per JOS-C46 + JOS-D0077: substrate components are the load-bearing
governance primitives — what JOS uses to declare structure, behavior,
and policy. They have their own folders (`rules/`, `principles/`,
`standards/`, `methods/`, `decisions/`, `concepts/`, `instruments/
{specs,gates,monitors,sops,...}`), their own `_index.yaml` files,
their own component-type manifests in `architecture/component-types/`.

A substrate component:
- Has an ID matching its substrate-type pattern (`JOS-R##`, `JOS-S##`,
  `JOS-D####`, etc.).
- Is enumerated in `.jos/registry.yaml` under its type section.
- Lives in canonical substrate-type folder (no mixing per SPEC-021).
- Is hand-authored. Persistent. Owned.

**Classification axis:** `type:` in frontmatter, one of the
substrate-type values per JOS-C46.

### Thing (what JOS governs)

Per JOS-D0066: thing components are what JOS GOVERNS in the world —
the units the substrate is APPLIED to. A venture is a thing. A
product is a thing. A system inside a venture is a thing.

A thing component:
- Carries an `object_id` per JOS-SPEC-007 universal base manifest.
  (Stays `object_id`, not `component_id` — the per-thing manifest
  field name was renamed `object_kind` → `component_kind` per
  JOS-D0077 rename batch, but the identifier field stays
  `object_id` for cascade compatibility.)
- Has a `_manifest.yaml` per JOS-SPEC-009.
- Lives in its own root folder OR is enumerated as a child via the
  hosting parent's manifest.
- Cascades through the JOS-D0049 universal base manifest pattern.

**Classification axis:** `component_kind:` in `_manifest.yaml`, one
of the 14 thing-type values per JOS-D0066. (See D-0066 §applicability
for which 9 of 11 thing-types carry lifecycle_phase.)

### Doc (the text files that document things)

Per JOS-D0054 + JOS-D0066: doc components are the human-readable text
artifacts that elaborate a thing's boundary contract. PURPOSE.md
declares why a thing exists; OVERVIEW.md declares what it is;
ARCHITECTURE.md declares how it works; etc. These elaborate the five
boundary-contract slots (Identity / Governance / Capability /
Interface / Lifecycle).

A doc component:
- Is a Markdown file with `<DOC-TYPE>.md` naming.
- Lives at the root of the Thing it documents (or in a slot folder
  per the thing-type's matrix).
- Carries frontmatter declaring its `doc_type:` (matches the file
  name).
- Required-or-optional per the thing-type × doc-type matrix in
  JOS-D0066.

**Classification axis:** `doc_type:` in frontmatter, one of the 11
doc-type values per JOS-D0054 + D-0066. (PRINCIPLES.md was added as
SUG-tier per Group A item 3.)

### View (the rendered projections)

Per JOS-SPEC-021 + JOS-C50: view components are read-only projections
of authoritative substrate. Registry is the canonical list (ONE per
repo); Indexes count per substrate folder (MANY); Manifests declare
per Thing (MANY); Directories project filtered cross-cutting digests
(MANY, per JOS-C50). The Registry / Index / Manifest / Directory
quartet.

A view component:
- Is either authoritative (Registry, Index, Manifest) or render-only
  (Directory).
- Is enumerated under its component-type folder (e.g., `directories/
  _index.yaml`).
- Follows strict no-mix rules per SPEC-021 — never confuse one
  view-kind for another.

**Classification axis:** view-kind, one of `registry | index |
manifest | directory`. (For Directory: also `template`, `sources`,
`filters`, etc. per JOS-SPEC-023.)

## Why "partition" not "tower"

The earlier framing during the 2026-05-19 riff was a "type tower" —
implying substrate underpins things, things have docs, docs are
viewed, like layers in a cake.

That framing is wrong for two reasons:

1. **It's a narrative, not a taxonomy.** The relationships between
   buckets are real (substrate IS applied to things; docs DO
   describe things; views DO project all the above), but those
   relationships don't put components into stacked tiers. A specific
   Method is not "below" a specific Venture; they're orthogonal
   citizens of the JOS world.

2. **It hides the classification-axis independence.** Substrate has
   its own type axis (per C46); Things have their own type axis (per
   D-0066); Docs have their own type axis (per D-0054); Views have
   their own type axis (per SPEC-021 + C50). Each axis is
   independent — the substrate-types do not imply or constrain the
   thing-types. Saying "tower" suggests vertical containment; the
   reality is horizontal partition.

The **partition** framing is MECE: every component is in exactly one
bucket; the four buckets together cover everything JOS knows about.
Easier to reason with, easier to teach, easier to defend against
"where does X go?" disputes.

## How agents use this concept

When an agent encounters a component X and needs to classify it:

1. **Ask "is X a structural primitive of JOS itself?"** If yes →
   Substrate. Look up its substrate-type per C46.
2. **Else ask "is X something JOS governs in the world?"** If yes →
   Thing. Look up its thing-type per D-0066.
3. **Else ask "is X a Markdown text artifact documenting a thing?"**
   If yes → Doc. Look up its doc-type per D-0054.
4. **Else ask "is X a rendered or canonical-listing artifact?"** If
   yes → View. Look up its view-kind per SPEC-021 + C50.

If none of the four fits, X is not a JOS component (yet); the partition
defines the universe. New kinds of components require extending one
of the four buckets — NOT introducing a fifth (the partition is
designed to absorb).

## Worked examples

| Component | Bucket | Axis value |
|---|---|---|
| `rules/JOS-R03-owner-required.md` | Substrate | substrate-type: rule |
| `instruments/methods/JOS-METHOD-DRAFT-A-RULE.md` | Substrate | substrate-type: method |
| `Foundry-Agent-System/_manifest.yaml` | Thing | thing-type: system |
| `Foundry-Agent-System/README.md` | Doc | doc-type: README |
| `architecture/_manifest.yaml` | View (per-thing manifest) | view-kind: manifest |
| `.jos/registry.yaml` | View (canonical registry) | view-kind: registry |
| `decisions/_index.yaml` | View (per-folder index) | view-kind: index |
| `.jos/directories/rules-meta.md` | View (rendered output) | view-kind: directory |
| `directories/JOS-DIR-RULES-META.md` | Substrate (declaration of a directory) | substrate-type: directory |

Note the last two rows — directories are interesting because the
DECLARATION lives in `directories/` (Substrate bucket, substrate-type
"directory") and the RENDERED OUTPUT lives in `.jos/directories/`
(View bucket, view-kind "directory"). Same kind, two roles. This
mirrors how a Spec lives in `instruments/specs/` (Substrate) while
the manifests it specifies live as `_manifest.yaml` per Thing (View).

The partition handles this cleanly: declarations are Substrate;
projections are Views.

## Edge cases + open questions

- **A `_manifest.yaml` IS a View component, but it's also hand-
  authored, not rendered.** Resolution per SPEC-021: manifests are
  authoritative-AND-view — they are the per-thing declared truth,
  consumed by tooling like views are consumed. The "View" bucket
  splits internally into authoritative views (Registry, Index,
  Manifest) and render-only views (Directory). C50 names this
  distinction.
- **Glossary terms — what bucket?** Glossary terms (in `glossary/
  core.yaml` etc.) are NOT JOS-tracked components in the same sense
  — they are vocabulary, not artifacts with their own frontmatter
  + lifecycle. They live alongside the partition, used by all four
  buckets. Tracked separately per SPEC-013 federation.
- **Evidence records (JOSW2 evidence trail)?** Per JOS-D0063, these
  are a separate per-event log, not components in the partition
  sense. They reference components but are not themselves in the
  partition.
- **Check-result files (per JOS-SPEC-022)?** Same as evidence —
  they are per-checker-run output, not components. They live in
  `check-results/<id>/` and are consumed by the Checkers Directory
  (JOS-DIR-CHECKERS), but they are not partition members.
